title: Paphiopedilum gardineri
intro: Paphiopedilum gardineri, also known as the Lady's Slipper Orchid, is a species of orchid native to the tropical forests of Borneo. It is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to alleviate conditions such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Paphiopedilum gardineri include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant has been used to make herbal preparations, including extracts and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
taxonomy: Paphiopedilum gardineri is a species of orchid native to the Philippines. Paphiopedilum gardineri belongs to the family Orchidaceae and the subfamily Cypripedioideae. It is a terrestrial orchid that grows in the wild in the Philippines' tropical forests. The species is also known to be part of the genus Paphiopedilum, which comprises around 80 species of Venus slipper orchids. Paphiopedilum gardineri is often classified as being part of section Brachypetalum.
